content:
Summary: Implemented a Supabase-backed workspace and chat flow. The app now loads real workspaces from Supabase, allows creating a workspace through a modal opened from a plus button in the sidebar and mobile header, and loads workspace-specific chat history through a workspace chat API. The chat send path now requires a valid workspace, validates that conversations belong to the selected workspace, and falls back to creating a conversation inside that workspace when needed. No SQL migration changes were required because the existing workspaces/conversations/messages schema already supported the flow.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Keep the page as the orchestrator and split persistence concerns into small API routes and repository helpers rather than embedding Supabase logic inside the page component.
2. [finished] Use a workspace-level active chat loader that fetches the latest conversation/messages for the selected workspace instead of storing mocked per-workspace chat state in the UI.
3. [finished] Require a valid workspace for chat submissions instead of auto-creating a default workspace in the chat route, so users are forced into explicit workspace creation.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Replaced mock workspace state with Supabase-loaded workspaces, added workspace creation flow, and wired the page to the new chat loader and modal. | Notes: Main UI orchestrator for the feature.
2. [modified] src/components/Sidebar.tsx — Sidebar now shows real workspace rows, adds a plus button for creating workspaces, and handles the empty-workspace state. | Notes: Desktop navigation entrypoint.
3. [created] src/components/CreateWorkspaceModal/index.tsx — New modal component for creating workspaces from the sidebar/mobile header CTA with validation and inline error handling. | Notes: Keeps workspace creation UI isolated.
4. [modified] src/lib/chat/useChat.ts — Hook now loads persisted workspace chat history from Supabase before streaming new messages and blocks sends while history is loading. | Notes: Client-side chat state and streaming logic.
5. [created] src/app/api/workspaces/route.ts — New API route to list workspaces and create a new workspace with validation. | Notes: Server boundary for workspace CRUD.
6. [created] src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/chat/route.ts — New API route to return the latest conversation and persisted messages for a workspace. | Notes: Server boundary for workspace-specific chat loading.
7. [modified] src/app/api/chat/route.ts — Chat streaming route now enforces valid workspace ownership for conversations and no longer falls back to the old auto-created default workspace flow. | Notes: Prevents chats from escaping the selected workspace.
8. [modified] src/lib/workspace/repository.ts — Added a listWorkspaces helper used by the new workspace listing API. | Notes: Keeps workspace data access centralized.
9. [modified] src/lib/chat/repository.ts — Added workspace conversation listing helpers used by the workspace chat loader. | Notes: Supports selecting the active workspace conversation.
10. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter/index.tsx — Added a neutral empty-state panel so the chat area still has a useful workspace-ready state when there are no messages. | Notes: Improves UX for newly created or empty workspaces.
11. [inspected] sqls/001_initial_schema.sql — Verified the existing schema already contained workspaces, conversations, and messages, so no migration was needed for this feature. | Notes: No file changes required.

content:
Summary: Adjusted the chat UI layout so the command center message list scrolls independently and the composer stays attached to the bottom without a separator line. The change was made primarily in the CommandCenter component, with a small parent layout tweak in the page to ensure the flex column can shrink correctly.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Keep the layout fix inside the existing CommandCenter component instead of extracting a new chat layout module, because the change is narrowly scoped to panel composition and scroll behavior.
2. [finished] Use flex column sizing with an internal scroll container rather than position: sticky/fixed for the composer, preserving the existing panel shell behavior while making the message list independently scrollable.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\canvas-ai\src\components\CommandCenter.tsx — Contains the message list and composer layout; updated to make the message list scrollable and remove the separator above the composer. | Notes: Added flex sizing and overflow handling to the message list wrapper and removed the composer top border.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\canvas-ai\src\app\page.tsx — Provides the chat panel container whose flex sizing must allow the internal scroll area to shrink properly. | Notes: Added min-h-0 to the chat panel wrapper and its sibling split panel wrapper.

The change was made primarily in the CommandCenter component, with a small parent layout tweak in the page to ensure the flex column can shrink correctly.

content:
Summary: Implemented a shared workspace settings system so the settings modal now controls real UI behavior across the app. The settings state was lifted to the page and threaded into the sidebar, artifact viewer, command center, and modal. Theme now updates the overall shell and panel styling, density changes spacing, font size adjusts artifact text/table rendering, line numbers can be toggled in the viewer, and auto-scroll can be disabled in the chat panel. The settings modal was also kept as a dedicated overlay component, with the About section removed earlier and the modal now reflecting the shared theme state.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Keep settings UI as a modal overlay component while moving the actual setting values to page-level shared state so multiple panels can respond consistently.
2. [finished] Use a small shared settings contract module instead of duplicating setting types across components.
3. [finished] Keep the modal content presentational and let the page own state mutation so future settings can be wired in one place.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Lifted settings state to the workspace page and passed shared settings into the sidebar, artifact viewer, and command center. | Notes: Also updated the mobile shell to reflect theme changes.
2. [modified] src/components/SettingsModal.tsx — Converted modal controls to receive shared settings state and dispatch updates instead of maintaining isolated local state. | Notes: The modal now reflects the app theme and density state.
3. [modified] src/components/Sidebar.tsx — Sidebar now passes the shared settings state into the modal so theme changes and control updates can affect the whole app. | Notes: The settings button still opens the modal.
4. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer.tsx — Artifact rendering now responds to shared line-number, font-size, theme, and density settings. | Notes: Text and sheet rendering both use the shared settings contract.
5. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter.tsx — Chat panel now respects shared theme, density, and auto-scroll settings. | Notes: Auto-scroll can be toggled off from the modal.
6. [created] src/components/workspaceSettings.ts — Introduced a shared typed settings contract to avoid duplicating setting shapes and to keep state updates explicit. | Notes: Defines the default workspace settings and updater type.

Theme now updates the overall shell and panel styling, density changes spacing, font size adjusts artifact text/table rendering, line numbers can be toggled in the viewer, and auto-scroll can be disabled in the chat panel. The settings modal was also kept as a dedicated overlay component, with the About section removed earlier and the modal now reflecting the shared theme state.
